channel.title: Kingson Wu
channel.description: Technical writing on software, AI systems, architecture, and learning.

item.title: Technology Advances, Distribution Lags: The Structural Contradiction of the AI Era
item.description: AI is approaching zero marginal cost for cognitive labor, decoupling economic growth from employment. This is not the same story as previous industrial revolutions.

item.title: Skills May Matter More Than Agents
item.description: Main AI vendors may be converging on a common pattern: the SDK provides the Runtime; developers own the Skills. A Skill is fundamentally an act of knowledge ext...

item.title: Harness Engineering Is Not a New Paradigm
item.description: Most people are treating Harness Engineering like it’s a new paradigm. It’s not. Start from first principles: an LLM takes input and produces output. The model...

item.title: From Custom Agents to Agent Runtime + Skill: Convergence in AI System Architecture
item.description: A systems-level view of how AI applications are evolving from bespoke agent implementations toward a layered architecture of interchangeable Runtime and reusable Skills.

item.title: MCP and Skills Solve Different Problems
item.description: MCP is basically a capability contract. It tells you what tools exist, how to call them, and what the permission boundaries are. A Skill is a different thing en...

item.title: AI Systems Need Method, Execution, and Evaluation
item.description: Most AI systems are still stuck in a single-inference pattern: they re-plan every time, discard execution results immediately, and accumulate no experience. A m...

item.title: Generative AI Does Not Think
item.description: Generative AI does not think. What it does is predict the highest-probability token given the preceding context. Humans can actively violate rules: “I know the...

item.title: AI Displacement Repeats Old Patterns
item.description: This wave of AI displacement shares familiar patterns with previous industrial revolutions. Technology moves forward, and some people get left behind before the...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Social Learning
item.description: AI is reshaping social learning. We once relied on peers to recommend resources, challenge ideas, and surface blind spots. Now more of those cognitive functions...

item.title: Expression Is Cheap, Taste Is the Edge
item.description: AI makes expression cheaper, but judgment becomes more important. Originality is shifting away from “who wrote the words” toward “who decided why this content s...

item.title: AI Is Combinational, Not Conceptual
item.description: Generative AI is not discovering new continents. It is a hyper-efficient search engine over high-dimensional probability space. It is strong at combinational in...

item.title: Engineering Cognition into Executable Form
item.description: We are not merely learning how to use AI. We are learning how to translate human tacit cognition into machine-interpretable, executable structures: thinking -&g...

item.title: Understanding Is Being Right Under Novelty
item.description: Perhaps we have misunderstood what understanding really means. Humans and large language models are alike in one crucial way: intuition comes first, reasoning f...

item.title: Intuition First, Explanation After: What Humans and LLMs Have in Common
item.description: A closer look at the shared tendency of humans and large language models to arrive at conclusions before generating plausible justifications — and what this means for how we understand "understanding."

item.title: Why Rust Makes Lifetime Annotations Part of the API
item.description: Rust lifetime annotations are not just compiler hints. They are part of the function contract, which is why explicit lifetimes matter for callers, traits, and API stability.

item.title: Why Temperature-0 LLM Inference Still Isn’t Deterministic
item.description: A practical look at why identical prompts can diverge under temperature=0, how batch-variant kernels create that behavior, and why batch-invariant operators matter for RL and reproducible experiments.
